title: Snap Q4 2021 Earnings Report
text:
Snap Q4 2021 Earnings Report

Financial Performance

Snap exceeded expectations in its fourth quarter 2021 results, reporting $1.3 billion in revenue, surpassing Wall Street's $1.2 billion projection and the company's own $1.1-$1.2 billion guidance. The quarter marked a significant milestone: Snap achieved $22.5 million in net income, representing the first quarter Snap reported positive net income since going public in 2017.

User Growth

Daily active users reached 319 million globally. Regional expansion showed strong momentum, particularly outside North America and Europe. The rest of the world segment expanded by 40 million users during the quarter and now represents 140 million of the total DAU count.

CEO Commentary

Snap CEO Evan Spiegel stated the company faced some fresh challenges in 2021 but posted strong results, reflecting substantial progress on our journey to sustainable growth and positive cash flow generation.

Forward Outlook

The company cautioned that Apple's iOS privacy changes would continue affecting advertising performance. CFO Derek Anderson noted it would take at least a couple more quarters for our advertising partners to build full confidence in our new measurement solutions.

Content and Platform Expansion

Snap renewed deals with Disney, ViacomCBS, and NBCUniversal while expanding its unscripted programming slate. The platform also pursued gaming growth through partnerships with Voodoo Games and Mojiworks.
